title: Rewrite dolt-remote-check in Python; unify conventions
description: The two utility scripts in this repo were written on different days with different tooling. bd-export-csv is Python (argparse, stdlib json, error: prefix), dolt-remote-check is Bash (manual case, python3 -c JSON one-liners, Error: prefix, banner-style help). Port dolt-remote-check to Python so both scripts share the same surface, eliminate the embedded python3 -c calls for JSON reading, and normalize CLI/help/error conventions across the repo. Also update CLAUDE.md to nail down error: (lowercase) as the one convention, replacing 'error: / Error:'.
design: Direction A (full Python rewrite). Shared bdutils.py module replaces the 'single self-contained executable' ethos — user explicitly accepted 'time for a new ethos' to favor DRY. Each script gets a 2-line symlink-safe prelude (sys.path.insert(0, str(Path(__file__).resolve().parent))) before 'from bdutils import ...' so ~/bin/ symlinks still work. Error prefix standardized to lowercase 'error:' matching Python convention; CLAUDE.md updated to drop '/ Error:' variant. Behavior change during port: OUT OF SYNC now exits 1 (was 0 in bash) so CI can gate on it. Script names unchanged (bd-export-csv vs dolt-remote-check) — different concerns, different prefixes. Old bash retained as dolt-remote-check.bash briefly for user smoke-test, then deleted in follow-up commit.
acceptance_criteria: dolt-remote-check is a Python 3 stdlib-only script; argparse with help examples; error: lowercase convention; same project-path handling as bd-export-csv (Path.expanduser().resolve(), no cd); output sections preserved; manual run verified on this repo; CLAUDE.md updated.
notes: Correction to design: ~/bin/ does NOT use symlinks — it holds forwarding stubs (commit edaa8dd in ~/bin repo) that print 'moved to github.com/deg/beads-utils' and exit 1. Scripts run from the repo directory, where Python's default sys.path[0] resolves the sibling bdutils.py. No symlink-safe prelude needed. Plain 'from bdutils import ...' suffices.
close_reason: Ported dolt-remote-check to Python; extracted bdutils.py shared helpers; DRY'd bd-export-csv; updated CLAUDE.md conventions; deleted old bash. Manual smoke-test passed (Python output byte-identical to bash; OUT OF SYNC now exits 1 as intended).
